sourceMovie.title: Home
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: People revisit Home for the same reason it broke through in 2015: clear stakes, memorable faces, and a comedy rhythm that doesn't stall in the middle. Tim Johnson keeps the ensemble pointed in one direction. The fantasy and comedy blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: Elio
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Home clicked for you, Elio (2025) is a strong next stop: both trade in comedy and animation, with comic timing and sharper banter. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after Home?
faq.answer: Try Elio, *batteries not included, Stitch! The Movie—each shares DNA with Home while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Home?
faq.answer: Home (2015) is best described as Fantasy, Comedy, Animation, Science Fiction, Family. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Home worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 6.8/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this fantasy lane.
